mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand across consumption and investment channels, boosting corporate profit forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month, or the signal loses predictive power during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates compresses real corporate margins and increases financing costs, creating a dual pressure that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and high-rate environment produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect reverses sign during stagflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports wage-driven consumption, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding the 2.5% threshold confirms robust economic expansion and strong corporate revenue fundamentals, which drives institutional portfolio rebalancing and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 20-day returns in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.5%, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment signals a supply-demand mismatch and economic contraction, triggering rapid risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative interaction signal fails to predict short-term drawdowns, or 5-day returns remain positive when both indicators cross their respective contraction thresholds simultaneously.
